across.clue: To see or know something that will happen in the future.
About to experience something pleasant | In for a ___
To discuss something in a general way and without dealing with the most important issues. | talk ___
To help a government, system, or organisation continue to exist, especially by providing financial or military support. | ___ up
A feeling of having no interest in or enthusiasm for anything.
Used to say that two choices or options are equally good, bad, or difficult; it makes no practical difference which you choose | it's as ___ as it's long
To try to hold, move, or find something using your hands in a way that is not skilful or graceful.
The best, most modern, or most impressive example of something | the ___ word in
To try to persuade someone to leave their job and go to work for another company.

down.clue: To feel bitter or angry about something, especially because you think it is unfair.
Punish someone as a warning | Make an ___ of
Dishonest or illegal behaviour by officials or people in positions of power.
Available for use by | At the ___ of
A child or other relative to whom you give food, money, and a home.
Not clear or definite; able to be understood in more than one way.
Something with very unusual features that make it very different from other things of its type.
